name: Client Report / 客户报告
description: Generate professional client-facing performance reports with portfolio returns, allocation breakdowns, and market commentary. Suitable for quarterly or annual distribution. Triggers on 'client report', 'performance report', 'quarterly report for [client]', 'generate reports', or 'client statement'.
steps: Step 1: Report Parameters - Gather client name, household, reporting period, accounts, benchmark, and firm branding
Step 2: Performance Summary - Produce household summary table with QTD/YTD/1-Year/3-Year/5-Year/ITD returns vs benchmark, and per-account breakdown
Step 3: Allocation Overview - Current allocation table with asset class, percentage, dollar value, and benchmark comparison
Step 4: Holdings Detail - Security-level detail with shares, price, value, portfolio weight, and quarterly return
Step 5: Market Commentary - Brief market summary tailored to client sophistication level
Step 6: Activity Summary - Trades, contributions, withdrawals, dividends, fees, and rebalancing activity
Step 7: Planning Notes - Progress toward goals, plan changes, action items, next review date
Step 8: Output - PDF report (8-12 pages) with firm branding, Word doc, optional Excel appendix

name: Client Review Prep / 客户会议准备
description: Prepare for client review meetings with portfolio performance summary, allocation analysis, talking points, and action items. Pulls together account data into a concise meeting-ready format. Use before quarterly reviews, annual checkups, or ad-hoc client meetings. Triggers on 'client review', 'meeting prep for [client]', 'quarterly review', 'prep for [client name]', or 'client meeting'.
steps: Step 1: Client Context - Gather client name, household members, account types, total AUM, IPS details, life stage, last meeting date
Step 2: Portfolio Performance - Performance tables (QTD/YTD/1-Year/3-Year/Since Inception) with attribution of top contributors and detractors
Step 3: Allocation Review - Current vs target allocation table with drift flags for IPS rebalancing threshold (3-5%)
Step 4: Talking Points - Generate meeting agenda: market overview, portfolio performance, allocation review, planning updates, action items
Step 5: Proactive Recommendations - Rebalancing trades, TLH opportunities, cash deployment, Roth conversion, beneficiary updates, insurance review
Step 6: Output - One-page summary, performance table, allocation pie chart, recommended actions, meeting agenda

name: Financial Plan / 财务规划
description: Build or update a comprehensive financial plan covering retirement projections, education funding, estate planning, and cash flow analysis. Use for new client onboarding, annual plan reviews, or scenario modeling. Triggers on 'financial plan', 'retirement plan', 'can I retire', 'education funding', 'estate plan', 'cash flow analysis', or 'plan update'.
steps: Step 1: Client Profile - Demographics, employment, accounts, income sources, expenses, liabilities, insurance, estate documents
Step 2: Cash Flow Analysis - Annual projections with inflation, tax rates, savings rate assumptions
Step 3: Retirement Projections - Accumulation and distribution phases with Monte Carlo simulation, sustainable withdrawal rate, RMDs
Step 4: Goal-Specific Analysis - Education funding (529 plans), estate planning (trusts, gifting), risk management (insurance needs)
Step 5: Scenario Modeling - Base case, early retirement, market downturn, higher spending, longevity, long-term care events
Step 6: Recommendations - Savings rate, asset allocation, tax optimization, insurance gaps, estate updates, beneficiary review
Step 7: Output - Financial plan document (15-25 pages), cash flow spreadsheet, retirement charts, goal analysis, action checklist

name: Investment Proposal / 投资提案
description: Create professional investment proposals for prospective clients. Covers the firm's approach, proposed allocation, expected outcomes, and fee structure. Use when pitching new clients or presenting a new investment strategy. Triggers on 'investment proposal', 'prospect presentation', 'pitch new client', 'proposal for [client]', or 'new client presentation'.
steps: Step 1: Prospect Context - Gather prospect details, current situation, assets, goals, risk tolerance, constraints, fee sensitivity, competition
Step 2: Proposal Structure - Build 6 sections: About Our Firm, Understanding Your Needs, Proposed Investment Strategy, Expected Outcomes, Fee Structure, Getting Started
Step 3: Customization - Match tone to prospect type, address concentrated stock, compare to robo-advisors if needed, lead with value for price-sensitive prospects
Step 4: Output - PowerPoint presentation (12-15 slides), PDF leave-behind, one-page email summary

name: Portfolio Rebalance / 组合再平衡
description: Analyze portfolio allocation drift and generate rebalancing trade recommendations across accounts. Considers tax implications, transaction costs, and wash sale rules. Triggers on 'rebalance', 'portfolio drift', 'allocation check', 'rebalancing trades', or 'my portfolio is out of balance'.
steps: Step 1: Current State - Capture account types, holdings, market values, cost basis, unrealized gains/losses
Step 2: Drift Analysis - Compare current allocation to IPS targets, flag positions exceeding rebalancing band (3-5%)
Step 3: Trade Recommendations - Generate trades with tax-aware rules: prefer tax-advantaged accounts, avoid short-term gains, harvest losses, watch wash sales
Step 4: Asset Location Review - Optimize placement: bonds/REITs in tax-deferred, growth assets in Roth, tax-efficient equity in taxable
Step 5: Implementation - Total trades, estimated costs, tax impact, net allocation drift effect
Step 6: Output - Drift analysis table, trade list (Excel), tax impact summary, before/after allocation

name: Tax-Loss Harvesting / 税损收割
description: Identify tax-loss harvesting opportunities across taxable accounts. Finds positions with unrealized losses, suggests replacement securities, and tracks wash sale windows. Triggers on 'tax-loss harvesting', 'TLH', 'harvest losses', 'tax losses', 'unrealized losses', or 'year-end tax planning'.
steps: Step 1: Identify Candidates - Scan taxable accounts for unrealized losses, prioritize by largest absolute loss, short-term losses first, largest % loss
Step 2: Gain/Loss Budget - Calculate realized gains/losses YTD, carryforward losses, target harvesting amount, estimate tax savings
Step 3: Replacement Securities - Suggest replacements maintaining similar exposure but not substantially identical (wash sale rule)
Step 4: Wash Sale Check - Check all household accounts for 30-day lookback and forward, verify no DRIP triggers
Step 5: Execution Plan - Trade list with sell/buy pairs, estimated proceeds, losses, replacement rationale
Step 6: Post-Harvest Tracking - After 30+ days, optionally swap back, update cost basis records
Step 7: Output - Harvest opportunity list (Excel), trade execution sheet, wash sale calendar, tax savings estimate

name: Accrual Schedule / 应计调度
description: Build the period-end accrual schedule — for each accrual, compute the entry, cite the support, and draft the JE. Use during month-end close; the JE is a draft for controller approval, not a posting.
steps: For each accrual on the policy list: derive accrual name, basis (contractual amount with source), period portion, already booked (from GL), this-period accrual
Draft JE: Dr expense account / Cr accrued liability with memo citing support reference
Note reversing entries if policy marks accrual as auto-reversing
Output: One table (the schedule) plus a JE draft block — do not post, staged for controller sign-off

name: Break Trace / 断点追溯
description: Root-cause a reconciliation break to its source transaction or posting — follow the audit trail from the break row back to the originating entry on each side and state what differs and why. Use after gl-recon has classified a break.
steps: Pull the GL side — fetch journal entry via internal-gl MCP: entry id, posting date, source system, batch id, preparer
Pull the subledger side — fetch matching transaction via subledger MCP: trade id, dates, counterparty, source feed, FX rate
Diff the attributes — line up posting date, FX rate/date, account mapping, quantity sign, amount sign
Write root cause as a single sentence: '<side> <did what> because <reason>'
Output JSON per break: key, root_cause, owner (ops/reference-data/accounting/upstream-system), expected_clear_date, action (monitor/adjust/raise-ticket/suppress)

name: GL Recon / 总账对账
description: Reconcile general ledger to subledger for a trade date or period — match at the position or transaction level, surface breaks, and classify each break by likely cause. Use for daily or month-end recon runs across asset classes.
steps: Step 1: Normalize both sides — align to common key and comparison columns, coerce types
Step 2: Match — full-outer-join on key, classify as Matched/Amount break/Quantity break/Timing break/GL only/Subledger only
Step 3: Classify likely cause — Timing, FX, Mapping, Duplicate/missing post, Fee/accrual, Data quality
Step 4: Output — Break report (one row per break, sorted by absolute delta) and Summary (counts/totals by bucket and cause)

name: NAV Tie-Out / NAV核对
description: Tie an LP statement to the fund's NAV pack — recompute the LP's capital account from the NAV components and flag any line that doesn't agree. Use before LP statements are distributed.
steps: Recompute the LP capital account: Beginning capital + Contributions - Distributions + Allocated net income - Carried interest = Ending capital
Pull each input from the NAV pack: LP commitment %, fund-level P&L, fees, expenses, waterfall outputs
Compare each line on the statement to recomputed value (tolerance: 0.01)
Additional checks: ending capital = next period beginning, sum of all LP capitals = fund NAV, commitment figures agree
Output: Pass/fail per line, recomputed vs statement values, list of flags

name: Roll-Forward / 滚动结转
description: Build a roll-forward schedule for a balance-sheet account — beginning balance plus activity less reversals equals ending balance, with each component tied to GL. Use for month-end close packages and audit support.
steps: Build roll-forward structure: Beginning + Additions + Accruals - Reversals - Payments +/- Reclasses +/- FX = Ending
Tie each line: Beginning from prior close, each activity line from GL query (cited), Ending from GL at period-end
Verify schedule must foot: all components must sum to ending balance
If gap exists, surface unexplained item (do not plug)
Output: Roll-forward table with 'ties to' column citing GL query/document per line, plus foot check (pass/fail)

name: Variance Commentary / 差异评述
description: Write flux commentary for every P&L and balance-sheet line over threshold — current vs prior period and vs budget, with the driver explained from underlying activity. Use for the month-end close package and management reporting.
steps: Apply threshold: flag lines where absolute variance >= materiality threshold (default 5%) or line is on 'always comment' list (revenue, headcount cost, cash)
For each flagged line: show Current/Prior/Budget values, delta vs prior and vs budget (amount and %)
Explain driver: one sentence explaining WHY (not restating the number) from underlying activity via internal-gl MCP
If driver unclear, write 'driver unclear — flag for controller'
Output: Commentary table plus short narrative (3-5 sentences) summarizing biggest movers

name: KYC Doc Parse / 文档解析
description: Parse an investor or client onboarding packet into structured KYC fields — identity, ownership, control, source of funds, and document inventory. Use as the first step of KYC screening; output feeds the rules engine.
steps: Step 1: Inventory the packet — list every document by type (identity, entity formation, ownership, address, source of funds, tax forms)

Step 2: Extract structured fields into JSON — applicant_type, legal_name, dob, nationality, address, id_documents, beneficial_owners, controllers, source_of_funds, pep_declared, tax_forms, documents_received. Use null for missing fields, never guess
Step 3: Flag obvious gaps — expired IDs, address proof older than 3 months, missing UBO chart for entities. These are inventory gaps for kyc-rules

name: KYC Rules / 规则引擎
description: Apply the firm's KYC/AML rules grid to a parsed onboarding record — assign a risk rating, list every rule outcome with the rule cited, and flag what's missing or escalation-worthy. Use after kyc-doc-parse; this skill decides nothing, it scores and routes.
steps: Step 1: Risk-rate — compute risk rating from grid factors: jurisdiction, applicant type, ownership opacity, PEP exposure, sanctions/adverse media, source of funds clarity. Output: low/medium/high with factor table
Step 2: Required-document check — list required docs for applicant_type at this risk rating, mark received/missing/expired
Step 3: Rule outcomes — for every applicable rule, output: rule id, rule text, outcome (pass/fail/n/a), evidence fields. Cite the rule always

Step 4: Disposition — output JSON with risk_rating, disposition (clear/request-docs/escalate-EDD/decline-recommend), missing_documents, escalation_reasons, rule_outcomes. This skill never approves — only escalators and humans do

name: Bond Relative Value / 债券相对价值
description: Perform relative value analysis on bonds by combining pricing, yield curve context, credit spreads, and scenario stress testing. Use when analyzing bond richness/cheapness, computing spread decomposition, comparing bonds, assessing bond value vs curves, or running rate shock scenarios.
steps: Step 1: Price the Bond(s) — call bond_price for target and comparison bonds, extract yield, Z-spread, duration, convexity, DV01
Step 2: Get Risk-Free Curve — call interest_rate_curve, interpolate at bond maturity for G-spread
Step 3: Get Credit Curve — call credit_curve for issuer country/type, extract credit spread, compute residual = G-spread minus credit curve
Step 4: Run Scenarios — call yieldbook_scenario with parallel shifts (-100bp to +100bp)
Step 5: Historical Context — call tscc_historical_pricing_summaries for spread percentile analysis
Step 6: Synthesize — combine spread decomposition, scenario results, historical context into rich/cheap assessment

name: Bond Futures Basis / 债券期货基差
description: Analyze the bond futures basis by pricing futures, identifying the cheapest-to-deliver, and comparing with yield curves to assess delivery option value and basis trading opportunities. Use when analyzing bond futures, computing the basis, identifying CTD bonds, calculating implied repo rates, or evaluating basis trades.
steps: Step 1: Price the Future — call bond_future_price, extract CTD, delivery basket, conversion factors, contract DV01
Step 2: Price the CTD Bond — call bond_price for CTD, extract clean/dirty price, yield, duration, DV01
Step 3: Compute Basis Metrics — gross basis, carry, net basis (BNOC), implied repo rate, compare to market repo
Step 4: Yield Curve Context — call interest_rate_curve for repo proxy
Step 5: Historical Context — call tscc_historical_pricing_summaries for 3M basis trend and percentile
Step 6: Sovereign Credit (optional) — call credit_curve for credit-driven distortions

name: Swap Curve Strategy / 互换曲线策略
description: Analyze the interest rate swap curve by pricing swaps at multiple tenors, overlaying government and inflation curves, and identifying curve trade opportunities. Use when analyzing swap curves, computing swap spreads, decomposing real rates, identifying steepener/flattener/butterfly trades, or comparing swap rates across currencies.
steps: Step 1: Discover Swap Templates — call ir_swap in list mode for target currency
Step 2: Build Swap Curve — call ir_swap in price mode for standard tenors (2Y, 5Y, 7Y, 10Y, 20Y, 30Y)
Step 3: Overlay Government Curve — call interest_rate_curve, compute swap spread at each tenor
Step 4: Inflation Decomposition — call inflation_curve, compute real rate = nominal swap rate minus inflation breakeven
Step 5: Compute Curve Metrics — 2s10s slope, 5s30s slope, 2s5s10s butterfly, curve shape classification
Step 6: Synthesize — swap curve table, spreads, real rate decomposition, curve metrics, trade recommendations with DV01-neutral sizing

name: FX Carry Trade / FX套利
description: Evaluate FX carry trade opportunities by combining spot rates, forward points, interest rate differentials, volatility surface analysis, and historical price trends. Use when analyzing carry trades, comparing FX forward curves, assessing carry-to-vol ratios, or evaluating currency pair opportunities.
steps: Step 1: Get Spot Rate — call fx_spot_price for currency pair, note bid-ask spread
Step 2: Price the Forward — call fx_forward_price at target tenor, compute annualized carry
Step 3: Map Carry Curve — call fx_forward_curve for all tenors, identify sweet-spot tenor
Step 4: Assess Vol Risk — call fx_vol_surface, extract ATM vol, 25d risk reversal, butterfly, compute carry-to-vol ratio
Step 5: Historical Context — call tscc_historical_pricing_summaries for 1Y daily data, assess 52-week range and trend
Step 6: Synthesize — carry profile table, vol surface summary, carry trade recommendation with conviction level

name: Option Volatility Analysis / 期权波动率分析
description: Analyze option volatility by combining vol surface data, option pricing with Greeks, and historical price data to assess implied vs realized volatility. Use when pricing options, analyzing volatility surfaces, computing Greeks, assessing vol premiums, or evaluating vol trading strategies.
steps: Step 1: Vol Surface Snapshot — call equity_vol_surface or fx_vol_surface, extract ATM vol term structure, 25d risk reversals, butterflies
Step 2: Template Discovery — call option_template_list for valid expiries and strikes
Step 3: Option Pricing — call option_value for specific options, extract premium, delta, gamma, vega, theta, implied vol
Step 4: Historical Data — call tscc_historical_pricing_summaries for 1Y daily history
Step 5: Realized Vol Computation — compute realized vol over 20d/60d/90d windows, compare to implied
Step 6: Synthesize — vol surface summary, Greeks table, implied vs realized comparison, strategy recommendations

name: Macro Rates Monitor / 宏观利率监控
description: Build macroeconomic and rates dashboards combining macro indicators, yield curves, inflation breakevens, and swap rates. Use when monitoring macro conditions, analyzing yield curve shape, decomposing real vs nominal rates, assessing policy rate expectations, or evaluating financial conditions.
steps: Step 1: Pull Macro Indicators — call qa_macroeconomic for GDP, CPI/PCE, unemployment, PMI
Step 2: Yield Curve Snapshot — call interest_rate_curve for government curve, compute 2s10s and 3M-10Y slopes
Step 3: Inflation Decomposition — call inflation_curve, compute real rates at each tenor
Step 4: Swap Spreads — call ir_swap at 2Y/5Y/10Y, compute swap spreads for financial conditions
Step 5: Historical Context — call tscc_historical_pricing_summaries for benchmark yield historical context
Step 6: Synthesize — macro summary table, yield curve snapshot, real rate decomposition, swap spreads, overall assessment

name: Equity Research / 股票研究
description: Generate comprehensive equity research snapshots combining analyst consensus estimates, company fundamentals, historical prices, and macroeconomic context. Use when researching stocks, comparing estimates to actuals, analyzing company financials, assessing equity valuations, or building investment cases.
steps: Step 1: Consensus Snapshot — call qa_ibes_consensus for FY1/FY2 estimates (EPS, Revenue, EBITDA, DPS)
Step 2: Historical Fundamentals — call qa_company_fundamentals for 3-5 fiscal years, extract growth, margins, leverage, ROE/ROIC
Step 3: Price Performance — call qa_historical_equity_price for 1Y history, compute YTD return, beta, 52-week range
Step 4: Recent Price Detail — call tscc_historical_pricing_summaries for 3M daily data
Step 5: Macro Context — call qa_macroeconomic for GDP, CPI, policy rate in company's primary market
Step 6: Synthesize — consensus tables, financials summary, valuation metrics (forward P/E), macro backdrop, investment thesis

name: Fixed Income Portfolio / 固收组合
description: Review fixed income portfolios by pricing multiple bonds, retrieving reference data, analyzing cashflows, and running scenario analysis. Use when reviewing bond portfolios, computing portfolio duration and DV01, analyzing cashflow waterfalls, stress testing rate scenarios, or assessing portfolio composition.
steps: Step 1: Price All Bonds — call bond_price for all holdings, extract yield, duration, DV01, convexity, spread
Step 2: Aggregate Portfolio Metrics — compute market-value weighted portfolio yield, duration, DV01, convexity
Step 3: Enrich with Reference Data — call yieldbook_bond_reference for sector, rating, maturity, currency breakdowns
Step 4: Project Cashflows — call yieldbook_cashflow, aggregate into quarterly cashflow waterfall
Step 5: Run Scenarios — call yieldbook_scenario with standard shocks (-200bp to +200bp)
Step 6: Curve Context — call interest_rate_curve for spread-to-curve context
Step 7: Synthesize — portfolio summary, composition breakdown, cashflow waterfall, scenario P&L table

name: Tear Sheet / 公司简报
description: Generate professional company tear sheets using S&P Capital IQ data via the Kensho LLM-ready API MCP server. Supports four audience types: equity research, investment banking/M&A, corporate development, and sales/business development. Produces audience-specific Word documents with company profile, financials, valuation, segments, earnings, and relationships.
steps: Step 1: Identify Inputs — company name/ticker, audience type (ER/IB-MA/CorpDev/Sales-BD), comparable companies (optional), page length preference
Step 2: Read Audience-Specific Reference — load the appropriate reference file for the audience type
Step 3: Pull Data via S&P Global MCP — company info, financials (4 fiscal years), segments, valuation, consensus, earnings, relationships, peers, M&A activity
Step 3b: Calculate Derived Metrics — margins, growth rates, efficiency ratios, capital structure, segment mix
Step 3c: Verify Data Files — check all intermediate files are present and populated
Step 4: Format as DOCX — apply style configuration, component functions, page length defaults, output to /mnt/user-data/outputs/

name: Earnings Preview / 盈利预览
description: Generate a concise 4-5 page equity research earnings preview for a single company. Analyzes the most recent earnings transcript, competitor landscape, valuation, and recent news to produce a professional HTML report with charts, tables, and full source traceability.
steps: Phase 1: Company Profile & Setup — parse ticker, get company info, next earnings date, business description
Phase 2: Earnings Transcript Analysis (MANDATORY) — get latest earnings transcript, extract verbatim quotes, guidance, key drivers, headwinds, Q&A themes
Phase 3: Competitor Analysis — get top 5-7 competitors, collect prices, EPS, market caps, consensus estimates
Phase 4: News & Sector Intelligence — Kensho Grounding searches for estimates, analyst ratings, risks, recent news, sector context
Phase 5: Financial Data Collection — 8 quarters of financials, segment data, earnings dates
Phase 6: Verification & Calculations — read ALL intermediate files, compute margins, growth rates, P/E ratios, verify cross-checks
Phase 7: Generate HTML Report — 4-5 pages with cover/thesis, estimates/themes/news, figures (8 charts/tables), appendix
Phase 8: Output — write HTML file, open in browser, summarize key findings

name: Funding Digest / 融资摘要
description: Generate a polished one-page PowerPoint slide summarizing key takeaways from recent funding rounds and notable capital markets activity across watched sectors or companies. Uses S&P Global Capital IQ data to produce professional deal flow briefings with valuation data and Capital IQ deal links.
steps: Step 1: Establish Coverage & Period — determine sectors, specific companies, time period (default: last 7 days)
Step 2: Build the Company Universe — seed companies from sector-seeds.md, pre-validate via get_info_from_identifiers, expand via competitors, validate expanded universe
Step 3: Pull Funding Rounds — get_rounds_of_funding_from_identifiers in batches, enrich with detailed round info, extract transaction_id, dates, amounts, valuations, investors
Step 4: Pull Company Context — get_company_summary for notable deals
Step 5: Identify Highlights & Trends — flag notable deals ($100M+, down rounds, new unicorns), identify trends, select 3-5 key takeaways
Step 6: Generate Company Logos — two-tier pipeline: simple-icons npm package or initial-based fallback via sharp
Step 7: Generate the One-Page PPTX — monochrome executive design, stat cards, key takeaways, top deals table with Capital IQ links
Step 8: QA the Slide — content QA (markitdown), visual QA (PDF conversion), link QA
Step 9: Present Results — copy to outputs, share file, provide verbal summary
